source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod si dixeris in corde tuo : Quare venerunt mihi hæc ? propter multitudinem iniquitatis tuæ revelata sunt verecundiora tua, pollutæ sunt plantæ tuæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si mutare potest Æthiops pellem suam, aut pardus varietates suas, et vos poteritis benefacere, cum didiceritis malum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et disseminabo eos quasi stipulam quæ vento raptatur in deserto.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc sors tua, parsque mensuræ tuæ a me, dicit Dominus, quia oblita es mei, et confisa es in mendacio.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Unde et ego nudavi femora tua contra faciem tuam, et apparuit ignominia tua :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: adulteria tua, et hinnitus tuus, scelus fornicationis tuæ : super colles in agro vidi abominationes tuas. Væ tibi, Jerusalem ! non mundaberis post me : usquequo adhuc ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod factum est verbum Domini ad Jeremiam, de sermonibus siccitatis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Luxit Judæa, et portæ ejus corruerunt, et obscuratæ sunt in terra, et clamor Jerusalem ascendit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Majores miserunt minores suos ad aquam : venerunt ad hauriendum. Non invenerunt aquam : reportaverunt vasa sua vacua. Confusi sunt, et afflicti, et operuerunt capita sua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propter terræ vastitatem, quia non venit pluvia in terram, confusi sunt agricolæ : operuerunt capita sua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nam et cerva in agro peperit, et reliquit, quia non erat herba.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et onagri steterunt in rupibus ; traxerunt ventum quasi dracones : defecerunt oculi eorum, quia non erat herba.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si iniquitates nostræ responderint nobis, Domine, fac propter nomen tuum : quoniam multæ sunt aversiones nostræ : tibi peccavimus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Exspectatio Israël, salvator ejus in tempore tribulationis, quare quasi colonus futurus es in terra, et quasi viator declinans ad manendum ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quare futurus es velut vir vagus, ut fortis qui non potest salvare ? Tu autem in nobis es, Domine, et nomen tuum invocatum est super nos : ne derelinquas nos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus populo huic, qui dilexit movere pedes suos, et non quievit, et Domino non placuit : Nunc recordabitur iniquitatum eorum, et visitabit peccata eorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixit Dominus ad me : Noli orare pro populo isto in bonum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cum jejunaverint, non exaudiam preces eorum, et si obtulerint holocautomata et victimas, non suscipiam ea : quoniam gladio, et fame, et peste consumam eos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixi : A, a, a, Domine Deus : prophetæ dicunt eis : Non videbitis gladium, et fames non erit in vobis : sed pacem veram dabit vobis in loco isto.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dicit Dominus ad me : Falso prophetæ vaticinantur in nomine meo : non misi eos, et non præcepi eis, neque locutus sum ad eos. Visionem mendacem, et divinationem, et fraudulentiam, et seductionem cordis sui, prophetant vobis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Idcirco hæc dicit Dominus de prophetis qui prophetant in nomine meo, quos ego non misi, dicentes : Gladius et fames non erit in terra hac : In gladio et fame consumentur prophetæ illi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et populi quibus prophetant erunt projecti in viis Jerusalem præ fame et gladio, et non erit qui sepeliat eos : ipsi et uxores eorum, filii et filiæ eorum : et effundam super eos malum suum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dices ad eos verbum istud : Deducant oculi mei lacrimam per noctem et diem, et non taceant, quoniam contritione magna contrita est virgo filia populi mei, plaga pessima vehementer.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si egressus fuero ad agros, ecce occisi gladio : et si introiero in civitatem, ecce attenuati fame. Propheta quoque et sacerdos abierunt in terram quam ignorabant.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid projiciens abjecisti Judam ? aut Sion abominata est anima tua ? quare ergo percussisti nos ita ut nulla sit sanitas ? Exspectavimus pacem, et non est bonum : et tempus curationis, et ecce turbatio.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cognovimus, Domine, impietates nostras, iniquitates patrum nostrorum, quia peccavimus tibi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ne des nos in opprobrium, propter nomen tuum, neque facias nobis contumeliam solii gloriæ tuæ : recordare, ne irritum facias fœdus tuum nobiscum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid sunt in sculptilibus gentium qui pluant ? aut cæli possunt dare imbres ? nonne tu es Dominus Deus noster, quem exspectavimus ? tu enim fecisti omnia hæc.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixit Dominus ad me : Si steterit Moyses et Samuel coram me, non est anima mea ad populum istum : ejice illos a facie mea, et egrediantur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod si dixerint ad te : Quo egrediemur ? dices ad eos : Hæc dicit Dominus : Qui ad mortem, ad mortem, et qui ad gladium, ad gladium, et qui ad famem, ad famem, et qui ad captivitatem, ad captivitatem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et visitabo super eos quatuor species, dicit Dominus : gladium ad occisionem, et canes ad lacerandum, et volatilia cæli et bestias terræ ad devorandum et dissipandum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dabo eos in fervorem universis regnis terræ, propter Manassen filium Ezechiæ regis Juda, super omnibus quæ fecit in Jerusalem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis enim miserebitur tui, Jerusalem, aut quis contristabitur pro te ? aut quis ibit ad rogandum pro pace tua ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tu reliquisti me, dicit Dominus ; retrorsum abiisti : et extendam manum meam super te, et interficiam te : laboravi rogans.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dispergam eos ventilabro in portis terræ : interfeci et disperdidi populum meum, et tamen a viis suis non sunt reversi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Multiplicatæ sunt mihi viduæ ejus super arenam maris : induxi eis super matrem adolescentis vastatorem meridie : misi super civitates repente terrorem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Infirmata est quæ peperit septem ; defecit anima ejus : occidit ei sol cum adhuc esset dies : confusa est, et erubuit : et residuos ejus in gladium dabo in conspectu inimicorum eorum, ait Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Væ mihi, mater mea ! quare genuisti me, virum rixæ, virum discordiæ in universa terra ? Non fœneravi, nec fœneravit mihi quisquam : omnes maledicunt mihi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dicit Dominus : Si non reliquiæ tuæ in bonum, si non occurri tibi in tempore afflictionis, et in tempore tribulationis adversus inimicum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid fœderabitur ferrum ferro ab aquilone, et æs ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Divitias tuas et thesauros tuos in direptionem dabo gratis, in omnibus peccatis tuis, et in omnibus terminis tuis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et adducam inimicos tuos de terra quam nescis, quia ignis succensus est in furore meo : super vos ardebit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tu scis, Domine : recordare mei, et visita me, et tuere me ab his qui persequuntur me. Noli in patientia tua suscipere me : scito quoniam sustinui propter te opprobrium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Inventi sunt sermones tui, et comedi eos : et factum est mihi verbum tuum in gaudium et in lætitiam cordis mei, quoniam invocatum est nomen tuum super me, Domine Deus exercituum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non sedi in concilio ludentium, et gloriatus sum a facie manus tuæ : solus sedebam, quoniam comminatione replesti me.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quare factus est dolor meus perpetuus, et plaga mea desperabilis renuit curari ? facta est mihi quasi mendacium aquarum infidelium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propter hoc hæc dicit Dominus : Si converteris, convertam te, et ante faciem meam stabis : et si separaveris pretiosum a vili, quasi os meum eris : convertentur ipsi ad te, et tu non converteris ad eos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dabo te populo huic in murum æreum fortem : et bellabunt adversum te, et non prævalebunt, quia ego tecum sum ut salvem te, et eruam te, dicit Dominus :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et liberabo te de manu pessimorum, et redimam te de manu fortium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et factum est verbum Domini ad me, dicens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non accipies uxorem, et non erunt tibi filii et filiæ in loco isto.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia hæc dicit Dominus super filios et filias qui generantur in loco isto, et super matres eorum, quæ genuerunt eos, et super patres eorum, de quorum stirpe sunt nati in terra hac :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Mortibus ægrotationum morientur : non plangentur, et non sepelientur : in sterquilinium super faciem terræ erunt, et gladio et fame consumentur : et erit cadaver eorum in escam volatilibus cæli et bestiis terræ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc enim dicit Dominus : Ne ingrediaris domum convivii, neque vadas ad plangendum, neque consoleris eos, quia abstuli pacem meam a populo isto, dicit Dominus, misericordiam et miserationes.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et morientur grandes et parvi in terra ista : non sepelientur, neque plangentur, et non se incident, neque calvitium fiet pro eis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et non frangent inter eos lugenti panem ad consolandum super mortuo, et non dabunt eis potum calicis ad consolandum super patre suo et matre.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et domum convivii non ingrediaris, ut sedeas cum eis, et comedas, et bibas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia hæc dicit Dominus exercituum, Deus Israël : Ecce ego auferam de loco isto, in oculis vestris, et in diebus vestris, vocem gaudii et vocem lætitiæ, vocem sponsi et vocem sponsæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et cum annuntiaveris populo huic omnia verba hæc, et dixerint tibi : Quare locutus est Dominus super nos omne malum grande istud ? quæ iniquitas nostra, et quod peccatum nostrum, quod peccavimus Domino Deo nostro ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: dices ad eos : Quia dereliquerunt me patres vestri, ait Dominus, et abierunt post deos alienos, et servierunt eis, et adoraverunt eos, et me dereliquerunt, et legem meam non custodierunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sed et vos pejus operati estis quam patres vestri : ecce enim ambulat unusquisque post pravitatem cordis sui mali, ut me non audiat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ejiciam vos de terra hac in terram quam ignoratis, vos et patres vestri : et servietis ibi diis alienis, die ac nocte, qui non dabunt vobis requiem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propterea ecce dies veniunt, dicit Dominus, et non dicetur ultra : Vivit Dominus qui eduxit filios Israël de terra Ægypti,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: sed : Vivit Dominus qui eduxit filios Israël de terra aquilonis, et de universis terris ad quas ejeci eos : et reducam eos in terram suam, quam dedi patribus eorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce ego mittam piscatores multos, dicit Dominus, et piscabuntur eos : et post hæc mittam eis multos venatores, et venabuntur eos de omni monte, et de omni colle, et de cavernis petrarum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia oculi mei super omnes vias eorum : non sunt absconditæ a facie mea, et non fuit occultata iniquitas eorum ab oculis meis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et reddam primum duplices iniquitates et peccata eorum, quia contaminaverunt terram meam in morticinis idolorum suorum, et abominationibus suis impleverunt hæreditatem meam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Domine, fortitudo mea, et robur meum, et refugium meum in die tribulationis, ad te gentes venient ab extremis terræ, et dicent : Vere mendacium possederunt patres nostri, vanitatem quæ eis non profuit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid faciet sibi homo deos, et ipsi non sunt dii ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Idcirco ecce ego ostendam eis per vicem hanc, ostendam eis manum meam, et virtutem meam, et scient quia nomen mihi Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Peccatum Juda scriptum est stylo ferreo in ungue adamantino, exaratum super latitudinem cordis eorum, et in cornibus ararum eorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cum recordati fuerint filii eorum ararum suarum, et lucorum suorum, lignorumque frondentium, in montibus excelsis,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: sacrificantes in agro : fortitudinem tuam, et omnes thesauros tuos in direptionem dabo ; excelsa tua propter peccata in universis finibus tuis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et relinqueris sola ab hæreditate tua, quam dedi tibi, et servire te faciam inimicis tuis in terra quam ignoras : quoniam ignem succendisti in furore meo : usque in æternum ardebit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus : Maledictus homo qui confidit in homine, et ponit carnem brachium suum, et a Domino recedit cor ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Erit enim quasi myricæ in deserto, et non videbit cum venerit bonum : sed habitabit in siccitate in deserto, in terra salsuginis et inhabitabili.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Benedictus vir qui confidit in Domino, et erit Dominus fiducia ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et erit quasi lignum quod transplantatur super aquas, quod ad humorem mittit radices suas, et non timebit cum venerit æstus : et erit folium ejus viride, et in tempore siccitatis non erit sollicitum, nec aliquando desinet facere fructum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Pravum est cor omnium, et inscrutabile : quis cognoscet illud ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ego Dominus scrutans cor, et probans renes : qui do unicuique juxta viam suam, et juxta fructum adinventionum suarum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Perdix fovit quæ non peperit : fecit divitias, et non in judicio : in dimidio dierum suorum derelinquet eas, et in novissimo suo erit insipiens.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Solium gloriæ altitudinis a principio, locus sanctificationis nostræ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Exspectatio Israël, Domine, omnes qui te derelinquunt confundentur : recedentes a te, in terra scribentur, quoniam dereliquerunt venam aquarum viventium Dominum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sana me, Domine, et sanabor : salvum me fac, et salvus ero : quoniam laus mea tu es.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce ipsi dicunt ad me : Ubi est verbum Domini ? veniat :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et ego non sum turbatus, te pastorem sequens : et diem hominis non desideravi, tu scis : quod egressum est de labiis meis, rectum in conspectu tuo fuit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non sis tu mihi formidini : spes mea tu in die afflictionis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Confundantur qui me persequuntur, et non confundar ego : paveant illi, et non paveam ego : induc super eos diem afflictionis, et duplici contritione contere eos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus ad me : Vade, et sta in porta filiorum populi, per quam ingrediuntur reges Juda, et egrediuntur, et in cunctis portis Jerusalem :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et dices ad eos : Audite verbum Domini, reges Juda, et omnis Juda, cunctique habitatores Jerusalem, qui ingredimini per portas istas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus : Custodite animas vestras, et nolite portare pondera in die sabbati, nec inferatis per portas Jerusalem :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et nolite ejicere onera de domibus vestris in die sabbati, et omne opus non facietis : sanctificate diem sabbati, sicut præcepi patribus vestris.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et non audierunt, nec inclinaverunt aurem suam : sed induraverunt cervicem suam, ne audirent me, et ne acciperent disciplinam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et erit : si audieritis me, dicit Dominus, ut non inferatis onera per portas civitatis hujus in die sabbati : et si sanctificaveritis diem sabbati, ne faciatis in eo omne opus :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ingredientur per portas civitatis hujus reges et principes, sedentes super solium David, et ascendentes in curribus et equis, ipsi et principes eorum, viri Juda, et habitatores Jerusalem : et habitabitur civitas hæc in sempiternum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et venient de civitatibus Juda, et de circuitu Jerusalem, et de terra Benjamin, et de campestribus, et de montuosis, et ab austro, portantes holocaustum, et victimam, et sacrificium, et thus, et inferent oblationem in domum Domini.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si autem non audieritis me ut sanctificetis diem sabbati, et ne portetis onus, et ne inferatis per portas Jerusalem in die sabbati, succendam ignem in portis ejus, et devorabit domos Jerusalem, et non extinguetur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Verbum quod factum est ad Jeremiam a Domino, dicens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Surge, et descende in domum figuli, et ibi audies verba mea.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et descendi in domum figuli, et ecce ipse faciebat opus super rotam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dissipatum est vas quod ipse faciebat e luto manibus suis : conversusque fecit illud vas alterum, sicut placuerat in oculis ejus ut faceret.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et factum est verbum Domini ad me, dicens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid sicut figulus iste, non potero vobis facere, domus Israël ? ait Dominus : ecce sicut lutum in manu figuli, sic vos in manu mea, domus Israël.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Repente loquar adversum gentem et adversus regnum, ut eradicem, et destruam, et disperdam illud :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si pœnitentiam egerit gens illa a malo suo quod locutus sum adversus eam, agam et ego pœnitentiam super malo quod cogitavi ut facerem ei.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et subito loquar de gente et de regno, ut ædificem et plantem illud.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si fecerit malum in oculis meis, ut non audiat vocem meam, pœnitentiam agam super bono quod locutus sum ut facerem ei.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nunc ergo dic viro Juda, et habitatoribus Jerusalem, dicens : Hæc dicit Dominus : Ecce ego fingo contra vos malum, et cogito contra vos cogitationem : revertatur unusquisque a via sua mala, et dirigite vias vestras et studia vestra.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui dixerunt : Desperavimus : post cogitationes enim nostras ibimus, et unusquisque pravitatem cordis sui mali faciemus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ideo hæc dicit Dominus : Interrogate gentes : Quis audivit talia horribilia, quæ fecit nimis virgo Israël ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid deficiet de petra agri nix Libani ? aut evelli possunt aquæ erumpentes frigidæ, et defluentes ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia oblitus est mei populus meus, frustra libantes, et impingentes in viis suis, in semitis sæculi, ut ambularent per eas in itinere non trito,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ut fieret terra eorum in desolationem, et in sibilum sempiternum : omnis qui præterierit per eam obstupescet, et movebit caput suum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sicut ventus urens dispergam eos coram inimico : dorsum, et non faciem, ostendam eis in die perditionis eorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixerunt : Venite, et cogitemus contra Jeremiam cogitationes : non enim peribit lex a sacerdote, neque consilium a sapiente, nec sermo a propheta : venite, et percutiamus eum lingua, et non attendamus ad universos sermones ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Attende, Domine, ad me, et audi vocem adversariorum meorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid redditur pro bono malum, quia foderunt foveam animæ meæ ? Recordare quod steterim in conspectu tuo ut loquerer pro eis bonum, et averterem indignationem tuam ab eis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propterea da filios eorum in famem, et deduc eos in manus gladii : fiant uxores eorum absque liberis, et viduæ : et viri earum interficiantur morte : juvenes eorum confodiantur gladio in prælio :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: audiatur clamor de domibus eorum : adduces enim super eos latronem repente, quia foderunt foveam ut caperent me, et laqueos absconderunt pedibus meis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tu autem, Domine, scis omne consilium eorum adversum me in mortem : ne propitieris iniquitati eorum, et peccatum eorum a facie tua non deleatur : fiant corruentes in conspectu tuo ; in tempore furoris tui abutere eis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus : Vade, et accipe lagunculam figuli testeam a senioribus populi et a senioribus sacerdotum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et egredere ad vallem filii Ennom, quæ est juxta introitum portæ fictilis : et prædicabis ibi verba quæ ego loquar ad te.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dices : Audite verbum Domini, reges Juda, et habitatores Jerusalem. Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum, Deus Israël : Ecce ego inducam afflictionem super locum istum, ita ut omnis qui audierit illam, tinniant aures ejus,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: eo quod dereliquerint me, et alienum fecerint locum istum, et libaverunt in eo diis alienis quos nescierunt, ipsi et patres eorum, et reges Juda : et repleverunt locum istum sanguine innocentum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et ædificaverunt excelsa Baalim, ad comburendos filios suos igni in holocaustum Baalim : quæ non præcepi, nec locutus sum, nec ascenderunt in cor meum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propterea ecce dies veniunt, dicit Dominus, et non vocabitur amplius locus iste Topheth, et vallis filii Ennom, sed vallis occisionis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dissipabo consilium Juda et Jerusalem in loco isto, et subvertam eos gladio in conspectu inimicorum suorum, et in manu quærentium animas eorum : et dabo cadavera eorum escam volatilibus cæli et bestiis terræ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ponam civitatem hanc in stuporem, et in sibilum : omnis qui præterierit per eam obstupescet, et sibilabit super universa plaga ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et cibabo eos carnibus filiorum suorum et carnibus filiarum suarum : et unusquisque carnem amici sui comedet in obsidione, et in angustia in qua concludent eos inimici eorum, et qui quærunt animas eorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et conteres lagunculam in oculis virorum qui ibunt tecum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et dices ad eos : Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum : Sic conteram populum istum, et civitatem istam, sicut conteritur vas figuli, quod non potest ultra instaurari : et in Topheth sepelientur, eo quod non sit alius locus ad sepeliendum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sic faciam loco huic, ait Dominus, et habitatoribus ejus, et ponam civitatem istam sicut Topheth.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et erunt domus Jerusalem, et domus regum Juda, sicut locus Topheth, immundæ, omnes domus in quarum domatibus sacrificaverunt omni militiæ cæli, et libaverunt libamina diis alienis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Venit autem Jeremias de Topheth, quo miserat eum Dominus ad prophetandum, et stetit in atrio domus Domini, et dixit ad omnem populum :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum, Deus Israël : Ecce ego inducam super civitatem hanc, et super omnes urbes ejus, universa mala quæ locutus sum adversum eam, quoniam induraverunt cervicem suam ut non audirent sermones meos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et audivit Phassur filius Emmer, sacerdos, qui constitutus erat princeps in domo Domini, Jeremiam prophetantem sermones istos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et percussit Phassur Jeremiam prophetam, et misit eum in nervum quod erat in porta Benjamin superiori, in domo Domini.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque illuxisset in crastinum, eduxit Phassur Jeremiam de nervo, et dixit ad eum Jeremias : Non Phassur vocavit Dominus nomen tuum, sed Pavorem undique.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia hæc dicit Dominus : Ecce ego dabo te in pavorem, te et omnes amicos tuos : et corruent gladio inimicorum suorum, et oculi tui videbunt : et omnem Judam dabo in manum regis Babylonis, et traducet eos in Babylonem, et percutiet eos gladio.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dabo universam substantiam civitatis hujus, et omnem laborem ejus, omneque pretium, et cunctos thesauros regum Juda dabo in manu inimicorum eorum : et diripient eos, et tollent, et ducent in Babylonem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tu autem, Phassur, et omnes habitatores domus tuæ, ibitis in captivitatem : et in Babylonem venies, et ibi morieris, ibique sepelieris tu, et omnes amici tui, quibus prophetasti mendacium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Seduxisti me, Domine, et seductus sum : fortior me fuisti, et invaluisti : factus sum in derisum tota die ; omnes subsannant me.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia jam olim loquor, vociferans iniquitatem, et vastitatem clamito : et factus est mihi sermo Domini in opprobrium, et in derisum tota die.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixi : Non recordabor ejus, neque loquar ultra in nomine illius : et factus est in corde meo quasi ignis exæstuans, claususque in ossibus meis, et defeci, ferre non sustinens.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Audivi enim contumelias multorum, et terrorem in circuitu : Persequimini, et persequamur eum, ab omnibus viris qui erant pacifici mei, et custodientes latus meum : si quomodo decipiatur, et prævaleamus adversus eum, et consequamur ultionem ex eo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dominus autem mecum est, quasi bellator fortis : idcirco qui persequuntur me cadent, et infirmi erunt : confundentur vehementer, quia non intellexerunt opprobrium sempiternum, quod numquam delebitur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et tu, Domine exercituum, probator justi, qui vides renes et cor, videam, quæso, ultionem tuam ex eis : tibi enim revelavi causam meam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cantate Domino, laudate Dominum, quia liberavit animam pauperis de manu malorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Maledicta dies in qua natus sum ! dies in qua peperit me mater mea non sit benedicta !

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Maledictus vir qui annuntiavit patri meo, dicens : Natus est tibi puer masculus, et quasi gaudio lætificavit eum !

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sit homo ille ut sunt civitates quæ subvertit Dominus, et non pœnituit eum : audiat clamorem mane, et ululatum in tempore meridiano,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: qui non me interfecit a vulva, ut fieret mihi mater mea sepulchrum, et vulva ejus conceptus æternus !

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quare de vulva egressus sum, ut viderem laborem et dolorem, et consumerentur in confusione dies mei ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Verbum quod factum est ad Jeremiam a Domino, quando misit ad eum rex Sedecias Phassur filium Melchiæ, et Sophoniam filium Maasiæ sacerdotem, dicens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Interroga pro nobis Dominum, quia Nabuchodonosor, rex Babylonis, præliatur adversum nos : si forte faciat Dominus nobiscum secundum omnia mirabilia sua, et recedat a nobis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixit Jeremias ad eos : Sic dicetis Sedeciæ :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus Deus Israël : Ecce ego convertam vasa belli quæ in manibus vestris sunt, et quibus vos pugnatis adversum regem Babylonis et Chaldæos qui obsident vos in circuitu murorum : et congregabo ea in medio civitatis hujus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et debellabo ego vos in manu extenta, et in brachio forti, et in furore, et in indignatione, et in ira grandi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et percutiam habitatores civitatis hujus : homines et bestiæ pestilentia magna morientur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et post hæc, ait Dominus, dabo Sedeciam regem Juda, et servos ejus, et populum ejus, et qui derelicti sunt in civitate hac a peste, et gladio, et fame, in manu Nabuchodonosor regis Babylonis, et in manu inimicorum eorum, et in manu quærentium animam eorum : et percutiet eos in ore gladii, et non flectetur, neque parcet, nec miserebitur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ad populum hunc dices : Hæc dicit Dominus : Ecce ego do coram vobis viam vitæ, et viam mortis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui habitaverit in urbe hac morietur gladio, et fame, et peste : qui autem egressus fuerit, et transfugerit ad Chaldæos qui obsident vos, vivet, et erit ei anima sua quasi spolium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Posui enim faciem meam super civitatem hanc in malum, et non in bonum, ait Dominus : in manu regis Babylonis dabitur, et exuret eam igni.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et domui regis Juda : Audite verba Domini,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: domus David. Hæc dicit Dominus : Judicate mane judicium, et eruite vi oppressum de manu calumniantis, ne forte egrediatur ut ignis indignatio mea, et succendatur, et non sit qui extinguat, propter malitiam studiorum vestrorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce ego ad te, habitatricem vallis solidæ atque campestris, ait Dominus : qui dicitis : Quis percutiet nos ? et quis ingredietur domos nostras ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et visitabo super vos juxta fructum studiorum vestrorum, dicit Dominus : et succendam ignem in saltu ejus, et devorabit omnia in circuitu ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus : Descende in domum regis Juda, et loqueris ibi verbum hoc,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et dices : Audi verbum Domini, rex Juda, qui sedes super solium David : tu et servi tui, et populus tuus, qui ingredimini per portas istas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus : Facite judicium et justitiam, et liberate vi oppressum de manu calumniatoris : et advenam, et pupillum, et viduam nolite contristare, neque opprimatis inique, et sanguinem innocentem ne effundatis in loco isto.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si enim facientes feceritis verbum istud, ingredientur per portas domus hujus reges sedentes de genere David super thronum ejus, et ascendentes currus et equos, ipsi, et servi, et populus eorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod si non audieritis verba hæc : in memetipso juravi, dicit Dominus, quia in solitudinem erit domus hæc.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia hæc dicit Dominus super domum regis Juda : Galaad, tu mihi caput Libani, si non posuero te solitudinem, urbes inhabitabiles !

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et sanctificabo super te, interficientem virum et arma ejus : et succident electas cedros tuas, et præcipitabunt in ignem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et pertransibunt gentes multæ per civitatem hanc, et dicet unusquisque proximo suo : Quare fecit Dominus sic civitati huic grandi ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et respondebunt : Eo quod dereliquerint pactum Domini Dei sui, et adoraverint deos alienos, et servierint eis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nolite flere mortuum, neque lugeatis super eum fletu : plangite eum qui egreditur, quia non revertetur ultra, nec videbit terram nativitatis suæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia hæc dicit Dominus ad Sellum, filium Josiæ, regem Juda, qui regnavit pro Josia patre suo, qui egressus est de loco isto : Non revertetur huc amplius,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: sed in loco ad quem transtuli eum, ibi morietur, et terram istam non videbit amplius.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Væ qui ædificat domum suam in injustitia, et cœnacula sua non in judicio : amicum suum opprimet frustra, et mercedem ejus non reddet ei :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: qui dicit : Ædificabo mihi domum latam, et cœnacula spatiosa : qui aperit sibi fenestras et facit laquearia cedrina, pingitque sinopide.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid regnabis quoniam confers te cedro ? pater tuus numquid non comedit et bibit, et fecit judicium et justitiam tunc cum bene erat ei ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Judicavit causam pauperis et egeni in bonum suum : numquid non ideo quia cognovit me ? dicit Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tui vero oculi et cor ad avaritiam, et ad sanguinem innocentem fundendum, et ad calumniam, et ad cursum mali operis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propterea hæc dicit Dominus ad Joakim, filium Josiæ, regem Juda : Non plangent eum : Væ frater ! et væ soror ! non concrepabunt ei : Væ domine ! et væ inclyte !

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sepultura asini sepelietur, putrefactus et projectus extra portas Jerusalem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ascende Libanum, et clama, et in Basan da vocem tuam : et clama ad transeuntes, quia contriti sunt omnes amatores tui.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Locutus sum ad te in abundantia tua, et dixisti : Non audiam : hæc est via tua ab adolescentia tua, quia non audisti vocem meam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Omnes pastores tuos pascet ventus, et amatores tui in captivitatem ibunt : et tunc confunderis, et erubesces ab omni malitia tua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quæ sedes in Libano, et nidificas in cedris, quomodo congemuisti cum venissent tibi dolores, quasi dolores parturientis ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Vivo ego, dicit Dominus, quia si fuerit Jechonias filius Joakim regis Juda annulus in manu dextera mea, inde evellam eum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et dabo te in manu quærentium animam tuam, et in manu quorum tu formidas faciem, et in manu Nabuchodonosor regis Babylonis, et in manu Chaldæorum :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et mittam te, et matrem tuam quæ genuit te, in terram alienam, in qua nati non estis, ibique moriemini.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et in terram ad quam ipsi levant animam suam ut revertantur illuc, non revertentur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid vas fictile atque contritum vir iste Jechonias ? numquid vas absque omni voluptate ? quare abjecti sunt ipse et semen ejus, et projecti in terram quam ignoraverunt ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Terra, terra, terra, audi sermonem Domini.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus : Scribe virum istum sterilem, virum qui in diebus suis non prosperabitur : nec enim erit de semine ejus vir qui sedeat super solium David, et potestatem habeat ultra in Juda.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Væ pastoribus qui disperdunt et dilacerant gregem pascuæ meæ ! dicit Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ideo hæc dicit Dominus Deus Israël ad pastores qui pascunt populum meum : Vos dispersistis gregem meum, et ejecistis eos, et non visitastis eos : ecce ego visitabo super vos malitiam studiorum vestrorum, ait Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ego congregabo reliquias gregis mei de omnibus terris ad quas ejecero eos illuc : et convertam eos ad rura sua, et crescent et multiplicabuntur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et suscitabo super eos pastores, et pascent eos : non formidabunt ultra, et non pavebunt, et nullus quæretur ex numero, dicit Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce dies veniunt, dicit Dominus, et suscitabo David germen justum : et regnabit rex, et sapiens erit, et faciet judicium et justitiam in terra.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In diebus illis salvabitur Juda, et Israël habitabit confidenter : et hoc est nomen quod vocabunt eum : Dominus justus noster.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propter hoc ecce dies veniunt, dicit Dominus, et non dicent ultra : Vivit Dominus, qui eduxit filios Israël de terra Ægypti,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: sed : Vivit Dominus, qui eduxit et adduxit semen domus Israël de terra aquilonis, et de cunctis terris ad quas ejeceram eos illuc, et habitabunt in terra sua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ad prophetas : Contritum est cor meum in medio mei ; contremuerunt omnia ossa mea : factus sum quasi vir ebrius, et quasi homo madidus a vino, a facie Domini, et a facie verborum sanctorum ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia adulteris repleta est terra, quia a facie maledictionis luxit terra, arefacta sunt arva deserti : factus est cursus eorum malus, et fortitudo eorum dissimilis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propheta namque et sacerdos polluti sunt, et in domo mea inveni malum eorum, ait Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Idcirco via eorum erit quasi lubricum in tenebris : impellentur enim, et corruent in ea : afferam enim super eos mala, annum visitationis eorum, ait Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et in prophetis Samariæ vidi fatuitatem : prophetabant in Baal, et decipiebant populum meum Israël.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et in prophetis Jerusalem vidi similitudinem adulterantium, et iter mendacii : et confortaverunt manus pessimorum, ut non converteretur unusquisque a malitia sua : facti sunt mihi omnes ut Sodoma, et habitatores ejus quasi Gomorrha.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propterea hæc dicit Dominus exercituum ad prophetas : Ecce ego cibabo eos absinthio, et potabo eos felle : a prophetis enim Jerusalem egressa est pollutio super omnem terram.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum : Nolite audire verba prophetarum qui prophetant vobis, et decipiunt vos : visionem cordis sui loquuntur, non de ore Domini.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dicunt his qui blasphemant me : Locutus est Dominus : Pax erit vobis : et omni qui ambulat in pravitate cordis sui dixerunt : Non veniet super vos malum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quis enim affuit in consilio Domini, et vidit, et audivit sermonem ejus ? quis consideravit verbum illius, et audivit ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce turbo Dominicæ indignationis egredietur, et tempestas erumpens super caput impiorum veniet.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non revertetur furor Domini, usque dum faciat et usque dum compleat cogitationem cordis sui : in novissimis diebus intelligetis consilium ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Non mittebam prophetas, et ipsi currebant : non loquebar ad eos, et ipsi prophetabant.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si stetissent in consilio meo, et nota fecissent verba mea populo meo, avertissem utique eos a via sua mala, et a cogitationibus suis pessimis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Putasne Deus e vicino ego sum, dicit Dominus, et non Deus de longe ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si occultabitur vir in absconditis, et ego non videbo eum ? dicit Dominus. Numquid non cælum et terram ego impleo ? dicit Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Audivi quæ dixerunt prophetæ prophetantes in nomine meo mendacium, atque dicentes : Somniavi, somniavi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Usquequo istud est in corde prophetarum vaticinantium mendacium, et prophetantium seductiones cordis sui ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui volunt facere ut obliviscatur populus meus nominis mei, propter somnia eorum quæ narrat unusquisque ad proximum suum, sicut obliti sunt patres eorum nominis mei propter Baal ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propheta qui habet somnium, narret somnium : et qui habet sermonem meum, loquatur sermonem meum vere. Quid paleis ad triticum ? dicit Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid non verba mea sunt quasi ignis, dicit Dominus, et quasi malleus conterens petram ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propterea ecce ego ad prophetas, ait Dominus, qui furantur verba mea unusquisque a proximo suo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce ego ad prophetas, ait Dominus, qui assumunt linguas suas, et aiunt : Dicit Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce ego ad prophetas somniantes mendacium, ait Dominus, qui narraverunt ea, et seduxerunt populum meum in mendacio suo et in miraculis suis, cum ego non misissem eos, nec mandassem eis : qui nihil profuerunt populo huic, dicit Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si igitur interrogaverit te populus iste, vel propheta, aut sacerdos, dicens : Quod est onus Domini ? dices ad eos : Vos estis onus : projiciam quippe vos, dicit Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et propheta, et sacerdos, et populus qui dicit : Onus Domini : visitabo super virum illum et super domum ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicetis unusquisque ad proximum, et ad fratrem suum : Quid respondit Dominus ? et quid locutus est Dominus ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et onus Domini ultra non memorabitur : quia onus erit unicuique sermo suus, et pervertistis verba Dei viventis, Domini exercituum, Dei nostri.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dices ad prophetam : Quid respondit tibi Dominus ? et quid locutus est Dominus ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si autem onus Domini dixeritis, propter hoc hæc dicit Dominus : Quia dixistis sermonem istum : Onus Domini, et misi ad vos dicens : Nolite dicere : Onus Domini :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: propterea ecce ego tollam vos portans, et derelinquam vos, et civitatem quam dedi vobis et patribus vestris, a facie mea :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et dabo vos in opprobrium sempiternum, et in ignominiam æternam, quæ numquam oblivione delebitur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ostendit mihi Dominus : et ecce duo calathi pleni ficis, positi ante templum Domini, postquam transtulit Nabuchodonosor rex Babylonis Jechoniam filium Joakim, regem Juda, et principes ejus, et fabrum, et inclusorem, de Jerusalem, et adduxit eos in Babylonem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Calathus unus ficus bonas habebat nimis, ut solent ficus esse primi temporis : et calathus unus ficus habebat malas nimis, quæ comedi non poterant eo quod essent malæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixit Dominus ad me : Quid tu vides, Jeremia ? Et dixi : Ficus, ficus bonas, bonas valde : et malas, malas valde, quæ comedi non possunt eo quod sint malæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et factum est verbum Domini ad me, dicens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus Deus Israël : Sicut ficus hæ bonæ, sic cognoscam transmigrationem Juda, quam emisi de loco isto in terram Chaldæorum, in bonum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ponam oculos meos super eos ad placandum, et reducam eos in terram hanc : et ædificabo eos, et non destruam : et plantabo eos, et non evellam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dabo eis cor ut sciant me, quia ego sum Dominus : et erunt mihi in populum, et ego ero eis in Deum, quia revertentur ad me in toto corde suo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et sicut ficus pessimæ quæ comedi non possunt eo quod sint malæ, hæc dicit Dominus : Sic dabo Sedeciam regem Juda, et principes ejus, et reliquos de Jerusalem, qui remanserunt in urbe hac, et qui habitant in terra Ægypti.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dabo eos in vexationem, afflictionemque omnibus regnis terræ, in opprobrium, et in parabolam, et in proverbium, et in maledictionem in universis locis ad quæ ejeci eos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et mittam in eis gladium, et famem, et pestem, donec consumantur de terra quam dedi eis et patribus eorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Verbum quod factum est ad Jeremiam, de omni populo Juda, in anno quarto Joakim filii Josiæ regis Juda (ipse est annus primus Nabuchodonosor regis Babylonis),

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quod locutus est Jeremias propheta ad omnem populum Juda, et ad universos habitatores Jerusalem, dicens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: A tertiodecimo anno Josiæ filii Amon regis Juda, usque ad diem hanc, iste tertius et vigesimus annus, factum est verbum Domini ad me, et locutus sum ad vos, de nocte consurgens et loquens, et non audistis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et misit Dominus ad vos omnes servos suos prophetas, consurgens diluculo, mittensque : et non audistis, neque inclinastis aures vestras ut audiretis,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: cum diceret : Revertimini unusquisque a via sua mala, et a pessimis cogitationibus vestris, et habitabitis in terra quam dedit Dominus vobis et patribus vestris, a sæculo et usque in sæculum :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et nolite ire post deos alienos, ut serviatis eis, adoretisque eos : neque me ad iracundiam provocetis in operibus manuum vestrarum, et non affligam vos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et non audistis me, dicit Dominus, ut me ad iracundiam provocaretis in operibus manuum vestrarum, in malum vestrum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Propterea hæc dicit Dominus exercituum : Pro eo quod non audistis verba mea,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ecce ego mittam et assumam universas cognationes aquilonis, ait Dominus, et Nabuchodonosor regem Babylonis servum meum, et adducam eos super terram istam, et super habitatores ejus, et super omnes nationes quæ in circuitu illius sunt : et interficiam eos, et ponam eos in stuporem et in sibilum, et in solitudines sempiternas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Perdamque ex eis vocem gaudii et vocem lætitiæ, vocem sponsi et vocem sponsæ, vocem molæ et lumen lucernæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et erit universa terra hæc in solitudinem, et in stuporem : et servient omnes gentes istæ regi Babylonis septuaginta annis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque impleti fuerint septuaginta anni, visitabo super regem Babylonis et super gentem illam, dicit Dominus, iniquitatem eorum, et super terram Chaldæorum, et ponam illam in solitudines sempiternas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et adducam super terram illam omnia verba mea, quæ locutus sum contra eam, omne quod scriptum est in libro isto, quæcumque prophetavit Jeremias adversum omnes gentes :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quia servierunt eis, cum essent gentes multæ, et reges magni : et reddam eis secundum opera eorum, et secundum facta manuum suarum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quia sic dicit Dominus exercituum, Deus Israël : Sume calicem vini furoris hujus de manu mea, et propinabis de illo cunctis gentibus ad quas ego mittam te.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et bibent, et turbabuntur et insanient a facie gladii quem ego mittam inter eos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et accepi calicem de manu Domini, et propinavi cunctis gentibus ad quas misit me Dominus,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Jerusalem, et civitatibus Juda, et regibus ejus, et principibus ejus, ut darem eos in solitudinem, et in stuporem, et in sibilum, et in maledictionem, sicut est dies ista :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Pharaoni regi Ægypti, et servis ejus, et principibus ejus, et omni populo ejus :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et universis generaliter : cunctis regibus terræ Ausitidis, et cunctis regibus terræ Philisthiim, et Ascaloni, et Gazæ, et Accaron, et reliquiis Azoti :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et Idumææ, et Moab, et filiis Ammon :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et cunctis regibus Tyri, et universis regibus Sidonis, et regibus terræ insularum qui sunt trans mare :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et Dedan, et Thema, et Buz, et universis qui attonsi sunt in comam :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et cunctis regibus Arabiæ, et cunctis regibus occidentis, qui habitant in deserto :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et cunctis regibus Zambri, et cunctis regibus Elam, et cunctis regibus Medorum :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: cunctis quoque regibus aquilonis, de prope et de longe, unicuique contra fratrem suum : et omnibus regnis terræ quæ super faciem ejus sunt : et rex Sesach bibet post eos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dices ad eos : Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum, Deus Israël : Bibite, et inebriamini, et vomite : et cadite, neque surgatis a facie gladii quem ego mittam inter vos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque noluerint accipere calicem de manu tua ut bibant, dices ad eos : Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum : Bibentes bibetis :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quia ecce in civitate in qua invocatum est nomen meum ego incipiam affligere, et vos quasi innocentes et immunes eritis ? non eritis immunes : gladium enim ego voco super omnes habitatores terræ, dicit Dominus exercituum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et tu prophetabis ad eos omnia verba hæc, et dices ad illos : Dominus de excelso rugiet, et de habitaculo sancto suo dabit vocem suam : rugiens rugiet super decorem suum : celeuma quasi calcantium concinetur adversus omnes habitatores terræ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Pervenit sonitus usque ad extrema terræ, quia judicium Domino cum gentibus : judicatur ipse cum omni carne. Impios tradidi gladio, dicit Dominus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum : Ecce afflictio egredietur de gente in gentem, et turbo magnus egredietur a summitatibus terræ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et erunt interfecti Domini in die illa, a summo terræ usque ad summum ejus : non plangentur, et non colligentur, neque sepelientur : in sterquilinium super faciem terræ jacebunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ululate, pastores, et clamate, et aspergite vos cinere, optimates gregis : quia completi sunt dies vestri ut interficiamini, et dissipationes vestræ : et cadetis quasi vasa pretiosa.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et peribit fuga a pastoribus, et salvatio ab optimatibus gregis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Vox clamoris pastorum, et ululatus optimatum gregis, quia vastavit Dominus pascua eorum :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et conticuerunt arva pacis a facie iræ furoris Domini.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dereliquit quasi leo umbraculum suum, quia facta est terra eorum in desolationem a facie iræ columbæ, et a facie iræ furoris Domini.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In principio regni Joakim filii Josiæ regis Juda, factum est verbum istud a Domino, dicens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus : Sta in atrio domus Domini, et loqueris ad omnes civitates Juda, de quibus veniunt ut adorent in domo Domini universos sermones quos ego mandavi tibi ut loquaris ad eos : noli subtrahere verbum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si forte audiant, et convertantur unusquisque a via sua mala, et pœniteat me mali quod cogito facere eis propter malitiam studiorum eorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dices ad eos : Hæc dicit Dominus : Si non audieritis me, ut ambuletis in lege mea quam dedi vobis,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ut audiatis sermones servorum meorum prophetarum quos ego misi ad vos, de nocte consurgens, et dirigens, et non audistis :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: dabo domum istam sicut Silo, et urbem hanc dabo in maledictionem cunctis gentibus terræ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et audierunt sacerdotes, et prophetæ, et omnis populus, Jeremiam loquentem verba hæc in domo Domini.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque complesset Jeremias, loquens omnia quæ præceperat ei Dominus ut loqueretur ad universum populum, apprehenderunt eum sacerdotes, et prophetæ, et omnis populus, dicens : Morte moriatur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quare prophetavit in nomine Domini, dicens : Sicut Silo erit domus hæc, et urbs ista desolabitur eo quod non sit habitator ? Et congregatus est omnis populus adversus Jeremiam in domo Domini.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et audierunt principes Juda verba hæc, et ascenderunt de domo regis in domum Domini, et sederunt in introitu portæ domus Domini novæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et locuti sunt sacerdotes et prophetæ ad principes, et ad omnem populum, dicentes : Judicium mortis est viro huic, quia prophetavit adversus civitatem istam, sicut audistis auribus vestris.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ait Jeremias ad omnes principes, et ad universum populum, dicens : Dominus misit me ut prophetarem ad domum istam, et ad civitatem hanc, omnia verba quæ audistis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nunc ergo bonas facite vias vestras et studia vestra, et audite vocem Domini Dei vestri, et pœnitebit Dominum mali quod locutus est adversum vos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ego autem ecce in manibus vestris sum : facite mihi quod bonum et rectum est in oculis vestris.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Verumtamen scitote et cognoscite quod, si occideritis me, sanguinem innocentem tradetis contra vosmetipsos, et contra civitatem istam, et habitatores ejus : in veritate enim misit me Dominus ad vos, ut loquerer in auribus vestris omnia verba hæc.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixerunt principes et omnis populus ad sacerdotes et ad prophetas : Non est viro huic judicium mortis, quia in nomine Domini Dei nostri locutus est ad nos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Surrexerunt ergo viri de senioribus terræ, et dixerunt ad omnem cœtum populi, loquentes :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Michæas de Morasthi fuit propheta in diebus Ezechiæ regis Juda, et ait ad omnem populum Juda, dicens : Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum : Sion quasi ager arabitur, et Jerusalem in acervum lapidum erit, et mons domus in excelsa silvarum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Numquid morte condemnavit eum Ezechias rex Juda, et omnis Juda ? numquid non timuerunt Dominum, et deprecati sunt faciem Domini, et pœnituit Dominum mali quod locutus fuerat adversum eos ? Itaque nos facimus malum grande contra animas nostras.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Fuit quoque vir prophetans in nomine Domini, Urias filius Semei de Cariathiarim, et prophetavit adversus civitatem istam, et adversus terram hanc, juxta omnia verba Jeremiæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et audivit rex Joakim, et omnes potentes et principes ejus, verba hæc, et quæsivit rex interficere eum : et audivit Urias, et timuit, fugitque, et ingressus est Ægyptum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et misit rex Joakim viros in Ægyptum, Elnathan filium Achobor, et viros cum eo, in Ægyptum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et eduxerunt Uriam de Ægypto, et adduxerunt eum ad regem Joakim, et percussit eum gladio, et projecit cadaver ejus in sepulchris vulgi ignobilis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Igitur manus Ahicam filii Saphan fuit cum Jeremia, ut non traderetur in manus populi, et interficerent eum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In principio regni Joakim filii Josiæ regis Juda, factum est verbum istud ad Jeremiam a Domino, dicens :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc dicit Dominus ad me : Fac tibi vincula et catenas, et pones eas in collo tuo,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et mittes eas ad regem Edom, et ad regem Moab, et ad regem filiorum Ammon, et ad regem Tyri, et ad regem Sidonis, in manu nuntiorum qui venerunt Jerusalem ad Sedeciam regem Juda.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et præcipies eis ut ad dominos suos loquantur : Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum, Deus Israël : Hæc dicetis ad dominos vestros :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ego feci terram, et homines, et jumenta quæ sunt super faciem terræ, in fortitudine mea magna, et in brachio meo extento, et dedi eam ei qui placuit in oculis meis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et nunc itaque ego dedi omnes terras istas in manu Nabuchodonosor regis Babylonis servi mei : insuper et bestias agri dedi ei, ut serviant illi :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et servient ei omnes gentes, et filio ejus, et filio filii ejus, donec veniat tempus terræ ejus et ipsius : et servient ei gentes multæ et reges magni.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Gens autem et regnum quod non servierit Nabuchodonosor regi Babylonis, et quicumque non curvaverit collum suum sub jugo regis Babylonis, in gladio, et in fame, et in peste visitabo super gentem illam, ait Dominus, donec consumam eos in manu ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Vos ergo nolite audire prophetas vestros, et divinos, et somniatores, et augures, et maleficos, qui dicunt vobis : Non servietis regi Babylonis :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quia mendacium prophetant vobis, ut longe vos faciant de terra vestra, et ejiciant vos, et pereatis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Porro gens quæ subjecerit cervicem suam sub jugo regis Babylonis, et servierit ei, dimittam eam in terra sua, dicit Dominus, et colet eam, et habitabit in ea.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ad Sedeciam regem Juda locutus sum secundum omnia verba hæc, dicens : Subjicite colla vestra sub jugo regis Babylonis, et servite ei et populo ejus, et vivetis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quare moriemini, tu et populus tuus, gladio, et fame, et peste, sicut locutus est Dominus ad gentem quæ servire noluerit regi Babylonis ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nolite audire verba prophetarum dicentium vobis : Non servietis regi Babylonis : quia mendacium ipsi loquuntur vobis :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quia non misi eos, ait Dominus, et ipsi prophetant in nomine meo mendaciter, ut ejiciant vos, et pereatis, tam vos quam prophetæ qui vaticinantur vobis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ad sacerdotes, et ad populum istum, locutus sum, dicens : Hæc dicit Dominus : Nolite audire verba prophetarum vestrorum, qui prophetant vobis, dicentes : Ecce vasa Domini revertentur de Babylone nunc cito : mendacium enim prophetant vobis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nolite ergo audire eos : sed servite regi Babylonis, ut vivatis : quare datur hæc civitas in solitudinem ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et si prophetæ sunt, et est verbum Domini in eis, occurrant Domino exercituum, ut non veniant vasa quæ derelicta fuerant in domo Domini, et in domo regis Juda, et in Jerusalem, in Babylonem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)
